hypothesis: Self-limiting surface reactions in multi-precursor ALP systems exhibit emergent synergistic effects that can only be discovered when LLM agents are explicitly constrained to explore non-equilibrium transient states (e.g., partial surface coverage, intermediate temperature ramps during pulse sequences) rather than steady-state saturation conditions, leading to identification of kinetically-accessible reaction pathways that would be missed by both traditional optimization-focused experimentation and agents allowed to prematurely converge on familiar cyclic processes.
